folio.label: 114v
folio.l: Heere may ©e se , how excellent fraunchise
In wo~man is , whan they he~ narwe a vyse
Somö tyraunt is , as there be many oone
That hatú an herte , as hard as ony stone
Whicú wold han lete hi~ , sterue in the place
Wel rather , than haue gunt hiö hire grace
And hem reioysen , in here cruel pride
And rekke nat , to ben an homycide
This gentil Mayus , fulfilled of pitee
Rigút of hire hond , a &lbar;re maketú she
In whicú she gunted hiö , hire verray grace
There lakketú nougút , oonly , but day and space
There as she mygút , vn to his lust suffice
For it shal be , rigút as he wol deuyse
And whan she saw hire tyme , vp oon a day
To visite this Damyan , gotú this May
And softly this &lbar;re , doun she threst
Vnder his Pilewe , rede it if hi~ list
She taketú hi~ by the hond , and hard hi~ twist
So secrely , that no wygút it wyst
And bad hi~ be al hole , and fortú she went
To Ianuarie , whan that he for hire sent

note.notetxt: 812there is a cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: Vp risitú Damyan , the next morwe
Al passed is , his syknes , and his sorwe
He kembetú , he proyneth hi~ , and pyketú
He dotú , al that his lady lust , and lyketú
And eke to Ianuarie , he gotú as lowe
As euäe dide a dogge , for the bowe
He is so plesaunt , to euäy man
For craft is al , who so that do yt can
That euäy wygút is fayn , to speke hi~ good
And fully , in hise ladies grace , he stod
Thus lete I Damyan , a boute his nede
And in my tale , fortú I wyl procede
¢ So~me Clerkys holden , that felicite
Stant in delite , and therfore certeyne he
This noble Ianuarie , witú al his mygút
In honest wyse , as longetú to a knygút
Shope hi~ to lyue , ful deliciously
His housyngö is arrayed , as honestly
To his degree , was maked , as a kynges
A monges othere , of hise honest thynges
He mad a gardeyne , walled al witú stone
A fairere gardeyne , wot I no wher noone
For out of doute , I verraily suppose
That he that wrot , the Romauns of the Rose
